intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands a NestJS module as the composition and dependency boundary that organizes related controllers, providers, and imported/exported capabilities.
primaryQuestion: What is a Module in NestJS and what role does it play in application structure?
expectedSignals: Defines a module as a class annotated with @Module() metadata that groups related application parts.
Explains that modules organize controllers, providers, and dependencies around a feature or technical concern.
Mentions that modules create dependency visibility boundaries, so providers are not automatically visible everywhere.
Connects feature modules to maintainability, separation of concerns, and clearer ownership in larger applications.
Recognizes AppModule as the root module that composes the rest of the application.
redFlags: Describes a module as only a folder or file naming convention.
Thinks modules only exist for routing and have no impact on dependency injection.
Assumes providers registered in one module are globally injectable without imports or exports.
Cannot distinguish feature modules from the root AppModule.

scoringHints.description: Explains @Module metadata, composition role, dependency visibility, feature boundaries, and how AppModule composes feature modules.
Understands modules as grouping units but misses provider visibility or root-module composition detail.
Knows that modules exist but treats them as folders or boilerplate without architectural meaning.
Cannot explain the role of modules in NestJS application structure.

followUps.question: If you had Users, Orders, and Payments features, how would you split them into modules and why?
followUps.purpose: Force the candidate to apply module boundaries to a concrete application.

followUps.question: What changes when OrdersService needs to use UsersService from another module?
followUps.purpose: Check whether module boundaries are connected to dependency visibility.

followUps.question: Why is putting every controller and service into AppModule usually a poor long-term structure?
followUps.purpose: Probe maintainability and modular design judgment.

en.title: Role of a NestJS module
en.displayQuestion: What is a Module in NestJS and what role does it play in application structure?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands modules as composition and dependency boundaries, not just files.
guidance: Define a module through @Module() metadata and the parts it can group.
Explain how modules help structure features and dependency visibility.
Use a simple AppModule plus UsersModule example.
commonMistakes: Calling a module only a folder.
Ignoring the dependency injection boundary.
Putting everything in AppModule without a reason.

vi.title: Vai trò của Module trong NestJS
vi.displayQuestion: Module trong NestJS là gì và nó đóng vai trò gì trong cấu trúc ứng dụng?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu module là boundary để compose ứng dụng và quản lý dependency, không chỉ là file/folder.
guidance: Định nghĩa module thông qua metadata của @Module() và các thành phần nó gom nhóm.
Giải thích module giúp chia feature và kiểm soát visibility của dependency như thế nào.
Dùng ví dụ AppModule import UsersModule để minh họa.
commonMistakes: Nói module chỉ là một folder.
Bỏ qua vai trò boundary của dependency injection.
Đưa toàn bộ controller/service vào AppModule mà không có lý do.

ja.title: NestJS module の役割
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS の Module とは何で、アプリケーション構造においてどのような役割を持ちますか。
ja.displayIntent: module を単なるファイルではなく、構成と dependency boundary として理解しているかを確認する。
guidance: @Module() metadata と、それがまとめる要素を説明する。
feature 分割と dependency visibility に module がどう関わるか説明する。
AppModule と UsersModule の簡単な例を使う。
commonMistakes: module を folder としてだけ説明する。
dependency injection の境界を無視する。
理由なくすべてを AppModule に置く。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for @Module metadata, feature modules, shared modules, imports, exports, and module boundaries.

intent: Check whether the candidate can accurately distinguish imports, exports, providers, and controllers in @Module() and use them to reason about module wiring.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between imports, exports, providers, and controllers in @Module()?
expectedSignals: Explains providers as injectable classes or provider definitions instantiated by Nest's injector within the module context.
Explains controllers as route handlers registered in the module.
Explains imports as modules whose exported providers are needed by this module.
Explains exports as the subset of this module's providers or imported modules made available to importing modules.
Clarifies that imports do not expose every provider from the imported module, only what that module exports.
redFlags: Says imports are for importing TypeScript files rather than module dependencies.
Thinks exports expose controllers as routes to other modules.
Believes providers become available app-wide as soon as they are listed in any providers array.
Cannot explain why a service may be registered but still not injectable from another module.

scoringHints.description: Clearly differentiates all four @Module arrays and explains provider visibility through exports and imports with an example.
Correctly explains most metadata fields but is slightly vague about exports or re-exports.
Recognizes the names but confuses TypeScript imports with Nest module imports or providers with controllers.
Cannot use @Module metadata to explain dependency injection or route registration.

followUps.question: If AuthModule imports UsersModule, what exactly must UsersModule export for AuthService to inject UsersService?
followUps.purpose: Verify understanding of exports as module public API.

followUps.question: Where would you put UsersController and UsersService in UsersModule metadata?
Why does adding UsersService to AuthModule.providers create a different ownership problem?
followUps.purpose: Probe duplicate registration and module boundary discipline.

en.title: @Module metadata fields
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between imports, exports, providers, and controllers in @Module()?
en.displayIntent: Check accurate understanding of the four core @Module() metadata fields.
guidance: Explain each field by responsibility, not just by name.
Clarify that imports consume exported providers from other modules.
Clarify that exports define the module's public injectable API.
commonMistakes: Confusing Nest module imports with TypeScript import statements.
Assuming imports expose all providers from another module.
Putting controllers in exports to share routes.

vi.title: Các trường metadata trong @Module
vi.displayQuestion: Trong @Module(), imports, exports, providers và controllers khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có phân biệt đúng bốn trường metadata cốt lõi của @Module() hay không.
guidance: Giải thích trách nhiệm của từng field, không chỉ dịch tên.
Nói rõ imports dùng các provider đã được module khác export.
Nói rõ exports định nghĩa public API injectable của module.
commonMistakes: Nhầm Nest module imports với TypeScript import.
Tưởng import một module là lấy được toàn bộ provider bên trong.
Đưa controller vào exports để 'share route'.

ja.title: @Module metadata の各 field
ja.displayQuestion: @Module() における imports、exports、providers、controllers の違いは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: @Module() の主要 metadata field を正確に区別できるか確認する。
guidance: 各 field の責務を名前だけでなく説明する。
imports は他 module が export した provider を使うためのものだと説明する。
exports は module の public injectable API を定義すると説明する。
commonMistakes: Nest module imports と TypeScript import statement を混同する。
import すれば相手 module の全 provider が見えると思う。
route を共有するために controller を exports に入れる。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for imports, exports, providers, controllers, and module encapsulation.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands global modules, their appropriate use for stable cross-cutting providers, and the coupling risk of making dependencies globally visible.
primaryQuestion: What is a Global Module and when should you use it?
expectedSignals: Explains that @Global() makes a module's exported providers available without importing that module in every consumer.
Mentions that a global module should still export the providers it wants to expose.
Says global modules should usually be registered once, often in a root or core module.
Gives appropriate examples such as configuration, logging, database connection, or shared infrastructure helpers.
Warns against making feature modules global because it hides dependencies and increases coupling.
redFlags: Suggests marking every shared module global to avoid imports.
Forgets that global visibility applies to exported providers, not all internal providers.
Uses global modules to bypass poor module boundaries or circular dependencies.
Cannot describe any downside of global modules.

scoringHints.description: Explains @Global(), exported provider visibility, one-time registration, good cross-cutting examples, and maintainability trade-offs.
Understands global availability but only partially explains exports or coupling risks.
Knows @Global by name but treats it mainly as a shortcut to avoid proper imports.
Cannot explain when global modules are appropriate or risky.

followUps.question: What dependency visibility problem can a global module hide during code review?
Would you make UsersModule global? Why or why not?
followUps.purpose: Separate cross-cutting infrastructure from business feature modules.

followUps.question: If making a module global fixes a circular dependency, what should you inspect instead?
followUps.purpose: Discourage using global modules as an architectural bandage.

en.title: Global modules
en.displayQuestion: What is a Global Module in NestJS and when should you use it?
en.displayIntent: Check understanding of @Global(), exported providers, and the coupling trade-off.
guidance: Explain what @Global() changes in provider visibility.
Mention that only exported providers become available to other modules.
Give safe examples and explain why feature modules should usually stay explicit.
commonMistakes: Making every shared dependency global.
Assuming internal providers are exposed without exports.
Using global modules to hide bad module boundaries.

vi.title: Global Module
vi.displayQuestion: Global Module trong NestJS là gì và khi nào nên dùng?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về @Global(), exported providers và trade-off coupling.
guidance: Giải thích @Global() thay đổi visibility của provider như thế nào.
Nhấn mạnh chỉ provider được export mới available cho module khác.
Đưa ví dụ phù hợp và giải thích vì sao feature module thường không nên global.
commonMistakes: Biến mọi dependency dùng chung thành global.
Tưởng provider nội bộ tự động được expose dù không export.
Dùng global module để che module boundary kém.

ja.title: Global Module
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS の Global Module とは何で、いつ使うべきですか。
ja.displayIntent: @Global()、exported providers、coupling の trade-off を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: @Global() が provider visibility をどう変えるか説明する。
他 module から使えるのは export された provider だけだと述べる。
適切な例を挙げ、feature module は通常 explicit import にすべき理由を説明する。
commonMistakes: 共有 dependency をすべて global にする。
exports なしで内部 provider が公開されると思う。
悪い module boundary を隠すために global module を使う。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Global modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for @Global(), exported providers, one-time registration, and design warnings.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can separate HTTP/API orchestration in controllers from business logic and dependency orchestration in services.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between a Controller and a Service?
expectedSignals: Explains controllers as classes responsible for handling incoming requests and returning responses.
Explains services as injectable providers that contain business logic, data access orchestration, or reusable application operations.
Mentions that controllers should delegate non-trivial work to services instead of embedding domain logic.
Connects services to dependency injection and testability.
Recognizes that services can be reused by multiple controllers or providers when exported/imported correctly.
redFlags: Puts all validation, persistence, and business rules directly in the controller.
Says services are only database wrappers or repositories.
Cannot explain why services are easier to unit test than fat controllers.
Manually instantiates services inside controllers with new.

scoringHints.description: Clearly separates request/response orchestration from business logic and ties service injection to reuse and testing.
Understands controller-service separation but misses testability or reuse detail.
Has a rough idea but still places significant business logic inside controllers.
Cannot distinguish controller responsibilities from service responsibilities.

followUps.question: For POST /users, what code belongs in UsersController and what belongs in UsersService?
followUps.purpose: Force responsibility split with a concrete endpoint.

followUps.question: Why does moving business logic into a service improve testing?
followUps.purpose: Probe testability and dependency injection understanding.

followUps.question: What goes wrong when a controller directly talks to multiple repositories and external APIs?

en.title: Controller versus service
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between a Controller and a Service in NestJS?
en.displayIntent: Check separation of HTTP handling from business logic and reusable providers.
guidance: Describe controllers as HTTP entry points.
Describe services as injectable units of application or domain logic.
Explain why delegation improves reuse, testing, and readability.
commonMistakes: Putting all business logic in controllers.
Treating services only as database access classes.
Using new instead of dependency injection.

vi.title: Controller và Service
vi.displayQuestion: Controller và Service trong NestJS khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng tách HTTP handling khỏi business logic và reusable provider.
guidance: Mô tả controller là điểm vào xử lý HTTP request.
Mô tả service là injectable chứa application/domain logic.
Giải thích delegation giúp reuse, test và đọc code tốt hơn.
commonMistakes: Đưa toàn bộ business logic vào controller.
Xem service chỉ là lớp truy cập database.
Dùng new thay vì dependency injection.

ja.title: Controller と Service
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS における Controller と Service の違いは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: HTTP handling と business logic、reusable provider の分離を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: controller を HTTP entry point として説明する。
service を application/domain logic を持つ injectable として説明する。
delegation が reuse、testing、readability にどう効くか説明する。
commonMistakes: business logic をすべて controller に置く。
service を database access class としてだけ扱う。
dependency injection ではなく new を使う。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Controllers
sourceReferences.note: Used for controller request/response responsibility and route metadata.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Providers
sourceReferences.note: Used for provider/service registration and dependency injection.

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands how @Controller('users') creates a route prefix and combines with method decorators such as @Get(), @Post(), and @Get(':id').
expectedSignals: Explains that @Controller('users') sets a controller-level path prefix.
Shows that @Get() inside the controller maps to GET /users.
Shows that @Get(':id') maps to GET /users/:id.
Mentions that method decorators add route-specific HTTP method and path metadata.
Optionally notes that a global prefix such as /api would be prepended if configured during bootstrap.
redFlags: Thinks @Controller('users') only names the class or module.
Cannot combine controller-level prefix with method-level paths.
Confuses @Controller('users') with route parameter extraction.
Assumes the prefix registers routes even if the controller is not part of any module.

scoringHints.description: Correctly maps controller prefix plus method decorators to full routes and mentions module registration when relevant.
Understands prefix composition but misses global prefix or module registration nuance.
Can state that it is a prefix but cannot produce exact routes.
Does not understand NestJS routing metadata.

followUps.question: What URLs are produced by @Controller('users') with @Get(), @Post(), and @Get(':id')?
What else must happen before Nest actually mounts that controller's routes?
followUps.purpose: Connect route metadata to module registration.

followUps.question: If app.setGlobalPrefix('api') is configured, what is the full path for @Get(':id')?

en.title: Controller route prefixes
en.displayQuestion: How does @Controller('users') define route prefixes in NestJS?
en.displayIntent: Check route metadata composition between controller and method decorators.
guidance: Explain the controller-level prefix.
Map @Get() and @Get(':id') to exact URLs.
Mention module registration or global prefix if relevant.
commonMistakes: Thinking the string only names the controller.
Forgetting method decorators add HTTP method and path.
Ignoring that the controller must be registered in a module.

vi.title: Route prefix của Controller
vi.displayQuestion: @Controller('users') định nghĩa route prefix trong NestJS như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng hiểu route metadata giữa controller decorator và method decorator.
guidance: Giải thích prefix cấp controller.
Map @Get() và @Get(':id') ra URL cụ thể.
Nhắc tới module registration hoặc global prefix nếu liên quan.
commonMistakes: Tưởng chuỗi này chỉ là tên controller.
Quên method decorator thêm HTTP method và path.
Bỏ qua việc controller phải được đăng ký trong module.

ja.title: Controller の route prefix
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS で @Controller('users') は route prefix をどのように定義しますか。
ja.displayIntent: controller decorator と method decorator の route metadata 合成を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: controller-level prefix を説明する。
@Get() と @Get(':id') を具体的な URL に対応付ける。
必要なら module registration や global prefix に触れる。
commonMistakes: 文字列は controller 名だけだと思う。
method decorator が HTTP method と path を追加することを忘れる。
controller が module に登録される必要を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Controllers
sourceReferences.note: Used for @Controller route prefix and method route decorators.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Global prefix FAQ
sourceReferences.note: Used for optional global prefix context.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands providers as injectable dependencies managed by Nest's inversion-of-control container, not just services by another name.
primaryQuestion: What is a Provider in NestJS?
expectedSignals: Defines a provider as a class or provider definition that Nest can instantiate and inject.
Mentions common provider examples such as services, repositories, factories, guards, or custom clients.
Explains that providers are registered in a module's providers array or exposed through imported modules.
Connects @Injectable() and constructor injection to Nest's dependency injection system.
Recognizes custom provider forms such as useValue, useClass, useFactory, or injection tokens as a deeper example.
redFlags: Equates provider only with service and cannot discuss other provider types.
Thinks @Injectable() alone makes a class available everywhere without module registration.
Manually constructs dependencies instead of letting Nest inject them.
Cannot explain why provider registration is needed.

scoringHints.description: Explains provider registration, injection, @Injectable(), DI container ownership, and at least one custom provider pattern.
Understands services as providers and constructor injection but misses custom providers or module visibility.
Knows providers by name but cannot explain registration or injection mechanics.
Cannot describe providers as injectable dependencies managed by Nest.

followUps.question: What is the difference between adding @Injectable() to a class and adding that class to a module's providers array?
Can a database client or config object be a provider? How would you register it?
followUps.purpose: Probe broader provider concept beyond services.

followUps.question: What breaks if a controller calls new UsersService() directly?
followUps.purpose: Check DI lifecycle and dependency graph awareness.

en.title: NestJS providers
en.displayQuestion: What is a Provider in NestJS?
en.displayIntent: Check understanding of injectable dependencies managed by Nest's DI container.
guidance: Define provider as something Nest can instantiate and inject.
Explain module registration in providers.
Mention services and at least one custom provider example.
commonMistakes: Saying provider means service only.
Thinking @Injectable() is enough without module registration.
Bypassing DI with manual instantiation.

vi.title: Provider trong NestJS
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về dependency có thể inject và được DI container của Nest quản lý.
guidance: Định nghĩa provider là thứ Nest có thể khởi tạo và inject.
Giải thích việc đăng ký trong providers của module.
Nhắc tới service và ít nhất một ví dụ custom provider.
commonMistakes: Nói provider chỉ có nghĩa là service.
Tưởng @Injectable() là đủ dù chưa đăng ký trong module.
Bỏ qua DI bằng cách tự new instance.

ja.title: NestJS provider
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS における Provider とは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: Nest の DI container が管理する injectable dependency を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: provider を Nest が instantiate して inject できるものとして定義する。
module の providers への登録を説明する。
service と、少なくとも一つの custom provider 例に触れる。
commonMistakes: provider は service だけだと言う。
@Injectable() だけで module registration が不要だと思う。
手動で new して DI を迂回する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Providers
sourceReferences.note: Used for provider registration, @Injectable(), and constructor-based injection.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Custom providers
sourceReferences.note: Used for provider tokens and custom provider patterns.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can correctly share a service across modules by exporting it from the owning module and importing that module in consumers.
primaryQuestion: How do you share a Service across multiple Modules?
expectedSignals: Identifies the module that owns the service and registers it in that module's providers array.
Exports the service from the owning module's exports array.
Imports the owning module into each consuming module that needs the service.
Explains that importing modules share the same singleton provider instance by default.
Avoids duplicating the service in multiple modules' providers arrays unless intentionally creating separate provider contexts.
redFlags: Adds the same service to providers arrays in every consuming module.
Imports the service class directly in TypeScript and assumes Nest DI will resolve it.
Makes the service global without considering whether explicit imports are clearer.
Cannot explain why the service must be exported by its owning module.

scoringHints.description: Uses owner-module provider registration, exports, consumer imports, singleton semantics, and avoids duplicate ownership.
Knows export/import pattern but misses singleton or duplicate-provider nuance.
Can make it work by copying providers but does not understand module ownership.
Cannot describe the standard NestJS service sharing pattern.

followUps.question: UsersService is declared in UsersModule and AuthService needs it. What exact metadata changes are required?
What is the risk of putting UsersService in both UsersModule.providers and AuthModule.providers?
followUps.purpose: Probe duplicate provider instances and ownership confusion.

followUps.question: When would you choose a global module instead of explicit imports?
followUps.purpose: Check judgment around shared infrastructure versus feature services.

en.title: Sharing services across modules
en.displayQuestion: How do you share a Service across multiple Modules in NestJS?
en.displayIntent: Check correct export/import wiring for a shared service.
guidance: Identify the owning module for the service.
Export the service from the owning module.
Import the owning module wherever the service is consumed.
commonMistakes: Adding the same service to every module's providers.
Confusing TypeScript import with Nest module import.
Making the service global by default.

vi.title: Share Service giữa nhiều Module
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để share một Service qua nhiều Module trong NestJS?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách wiring export/import đúng để dùng chung service.
guidance: Xác định module sở hữu service.
Export service từ module sở hữu.
Import module sở hữu vào module nào cần dùng service.
commonMistakes: Thêm cùng một service vào providers của mọi module.
Nhầm TypeScript import với Nest module import.
Mặc định biến service thành global.

ja.title: module 間で Service を共有する
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS で Service を複数の Module 間で共有するにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: shared service の正しい export/import wiring を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: service の owning module を特定する。
owning module から service を export する。
service を使う module が owning module を import する。
commonMistakes: 同じ service をすべての module の providers に追加する。
TypeScript import と Nest module import を混同する。
デフォルトで service を global にする。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Shared modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for exporting a service from one module and importing that module in consumers.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can diagnose the common NestJS dependency resolution failure caused by missing provider registration or missing module exports/imports.
primaryQuestion: What happens if you forget to add a Service to the providers array?
expectedSignals: Explains that Nest may fail to resolve the dependency at application startup or testing module compilation.
Mentions common error wording such as Nest can't resolve dependencies of X or provider not available in the current context.
Distinguishes missing local provider registration from missing export/import across modules.
States that @Injectable() alone does not register the provider with a module.
Uses the constructor parameter index or injection token in the error message to locate the missing dependency.
redFlags: Says the service will just be undefined and fail later in business logic.
Fixes the problem by randomly adding the service to many modules.
Ignores module exports/imports and only checks TypeScript imports.
Cannot use the Nest error message to identify the unresolved dependency.

scoringHints.description: Explains DI resolution failure, startup/test compile timing, @Injectable versus registration, and systematic debugging of module context.
Knows a DI error occurs but misses cross-module export/import or error-index detail.
Can patch by trial and error but cannot reason from the module context.
Does not understand provider registration failure behavior.

followUps.question: Nest says it cannot resolve dependencies of OrdersService at index [1]. What do you inspect first?
How is the fix different if the missing dependency belongs to another module?
Why is adding the same provider to the consuming module not always the right fix?
followUps.purpose: Probe ownership and duplicate instance risks.

en.title: Missing provider registration
en.displayQuestion: What happens if you forget to add a Service to the providers array?
en.displayIntent: Check ability to diagnose NestJS dependency resolution errors.
guidance: Describe the DI resolution error and when it appears.
Explain @Injectable() versus module provider registration.
Separate local registration from cross-module exports/imports.
commonMistakes: Assuming the value will simply be undefined.
Adding providers randomly to many modules.
Only checking TypeScript imports.

vi.title: Thiếu provider registration
vi.displayQuestion: Điều gì xảy ra nếu bạn quên thêm một Service vào providers array?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng debug lỗi dependency resolution trong NestJS.
guidance: Mô tả lỗi DI resolution và thời điểm lỗi xuất hiện.
Giải thích @Injectable() khác gì với đăng ký provider trong module.
Tách trường hợp provider local với trường hợp cần exports/imports giữa module.
commonMistakes: Tưởng value chỉ bị undefined rồi lỗi sau.
Thêm provider bừa vào nhiều module.
Chỉ kiểm tra TypeScript import.

ja.title: provider registration の不足
ja.displayQuestion: Service を providers array に追加し忘れると何が起きますか。
ja.displayIntent: NestJS の dependency resolution error を診断できるか確認する。
guidance: DI resolution error と発生タイミングを説明する。
@Injectable() と module provider registration の違いを説明する。
local registration と module 間 exports/imports の問題を分ける。
commonMistakes: 値が単に undefined になると思う。
多くの module に provider をランダムに追加する。
TypeScript import だけを確認する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Providers
sourceReferences.note: Used for provider registration and dependency resolution.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for module-scoped provider visibility.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands AppModule as the root module passed to NestFactory and the composition point for application modules.
primaryQuestion: What is AppModule and why must every NestJS app have one?
expectedSignals: Defines AppModule as the root module of the Nest application.
Explains that main.ts bootstraps the application with NestFactory.create(AppModule).
Says AppModule composes feature modules through imports and may configure application-wide infrastructure.
Clarifies that controllers and providers can live in feature modules imported by AppModule, not necessarily directly inside AppModule.
Recognizes that Nest needs a root module to build the module graph and dependency injection container.
redFlags: Thinks AppModule is just a default generated file that can be deleted without replacement.
Puts all controllers and services directly in AppModule because it is the root.
Cannot connect AppModule to NestFactory bootstrap.
Confuses AppModule with main.ts or an HTTP controller.

scoringHints.description: Explains bootstrap, root module graph, feature-module composition, and appropriate AppModule responsibilities.
Understands AppModule as root but misses composition or DI graph detail.
Recognizes the name but treats it as generated boilerplate.
Cannot explain why a Nest application needs a root module.

followUps.question: What does NestFactory.create(AppModule) need AppModule for?
followUps.purpose: Connect bootstrap to module graph construction.

followUps.question: What should AppModule import in an app with UsersModule and OrdersModule?
Why is AppModule usually not the right place for every feature service?

en.title: AppModule as root module
en.displayQuestion: What is AppModule and why must every NestJS app have one?
en.displayIntent: Check root module, bootstrap, and module graph understanding.
guidance: Explain AppModule as the root module.
Connect it to NestFactory.create(AppModule).
Describe how it composes feature modules.
commonMistakes: Treating AppModule as disposable boilerplate.
Putting every feature directly in AppModule.
Confusing AppModule with main.ts.

vi.title: AppModule là root module
vi.displayQuestion: AppModule là gì và vì sao mỗi ứng dụng NestJS phải có một root module?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về root module, bootstrap và module graph.
guidance: Giải thích AppModule là root module.
Liên hệ với NestFactory.create(AppModule).
Mô tả cách AppModule compose các feature module.
commonMistakes: Xem AppModule là boilerplate có thể bỏ qua.
Đưa mọi feature trực tiếp vào AppModule.
Nhầm AppModule với main.ts.

ja.title: root module としての AppModule
ja.displayQuestion: AppModule とは何で、なぜすべての NestJS app に必要ですか。
ja.displayIntent: root module、bootstrap、module graph を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: AppModule を root module として説明する。
NestFactory.create(AppModule) と関連付ける。
feature module を compose する役割を説明する。
commonMistakes: AppModule を不要な boilerplate として扱う。
すべての feature を直接 AppModule に置く。
AppModule と main.ts を混同する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: First steps
sourceReferences.note: Used for NestFactory.create(AppModule) bootstrap pattern.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for root module and feature module composition.

intent: Verify whether the candidate can use NestJS parameter decorators to extract route parameters, query strings, and request bodies in controller handlers.
primaryQuestion: How do you extract route params, query strings, and request body in a Controller?
expectedSignals: Uses @Param('id') to extract route parameters from a path such as @Get(':id').
Uses @Query() or @Query('limit') to read query string values.
Uses @Body() or @Body('field') to read request payloads.
Mentions DTO classes for typed request bodies and validation with pipes when appropriate.
Understands that decorator values are handler parameters and should be passed to services after validation or transformation.
redFlags: Accesses raw req manually for every endpoint instead of using Nest parameter decorators.
Confuses @Param and @Query or cannot produce a route example.
Uses untyped any payloads without mentioning DTOs or validation.
Parses route params from strings manually in business logic without considering pipes.

scoringHints.description: Shows correct @Param, @Query, @Body usage with concrete route examples and mentions DTO validation or pipes.
Uses the decorators correctly but misses DTO validation or primitive transformation.
Knows some decorator names but confuses when to use each.
Cannot explain how controller handler inputs are extracted in NestJS.

followUps.question: Write the handler signature for PUT /users/:id?notify=true with an UpdateUserDto body.
How would you ensure id is a number before the service receives it?
When would you still use @Req() instead of @Param, @Query, and @Body?
followUps.purpose: Check understanding of standard versus raw request access.

en.title: Controller input decorators
en.displayQuestion: How do you extract route params, query strings, and request body in a NestJS Controller?
en.displayIntent: Check practical use of @Param, @Query, @Body, DTOs, and pipes.
guidance: Map route params to @Param().
Map query strings to @Query().
Map request bodies to @Body() and mention DTO validation.
commonMistakes: Using raw request access for normal cases.
Confusing route params with query strings.
Skipping DTOs and validation.

vi.title: Decorator lấy input trong Controller
vi.displayQuestion: Trong Controller NestJS, bạn lấy route params, query strings và request body như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng dùng @Param, @Query, @Body, DTO và pipe trong thực tế.
guidance: Map route params bằng @Param().
Map query strings bằng @Query().
Map request body bằng @Body() và nhắc tới DTO validation.
commonMistakes: Dùng raw request cho các case thông thường.
Nhầm route param với query string.
Bỏ qua DTO và validation.

ja.title: Controller input decorator
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS Controller で route params、query strings、request body をどう取り出しますか。
ja.displayIntent: @Param、@Query、@Body、DTO、pipe の実践的な使い方を確認する。
guidance: route params を @Param() に対応付ける。
query strings を @Query() に対応付ける。
request body を @Body() に対応付け、DTO validation に触れる。
commonMistakes: 通常ケースで raw request を使う。
route param と query string を混同する。
DTO と validation を省略する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Controllers
sourceReferences.note: Used for @Param, @Query, @Body, DTO, and request handler examples.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Pipes
sourceReferences.note: Used for route parameter transformation and validation context.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands feature modules as domain-oriented organization units that improve cohesion, maintainability, and dependency boundaries.
primaryQuestion: What is a Feature Module and what are its benefits?
expectedSignals: Defines a feature module as a module grouping code for a specific business capability or feature.
Uses examples such as UsersModule containing UsersController, UsersService, DTOs, and related providers.
Explains benefits such as cohesion, easier navigation, clearer ownership, and isolated module boundaries.
Mentions that AppModule can import feature modules to compose the application.
Recognizes that feature modules should export only the providers other modules actually need.
redFlags: Splits modules only by technical layer such as one giant ControllersModule and one giant ServicesModule.
Creates feature modules but exports everything from them by default.
Does not connect feature modules to dependency boundaries.
Cannot explain a benefit beyond generated file organization.

scoringHints.description: Explains domain cohesion, feature ownership, AppModule composition, explicit exports, and maintainability benefits.
Understands feature grouping but misses exports or dependency boundary implications.
Treats feature modules as folders generated by CLI without architectural benefits.
Cannot explain what feature modules are for.

followUps.question: What would you put inside UsersModule and what would you keep outside it?
When can splitting too aggressively into modules become painful?
Why is a giant SharedModule not the same as healthy feature modularization?
followUps.purpose: Check understanding of cohesion and public API control.

en.title: Feature modules
en.displayQuestion: What is a Feature Module in NestJS and what are its benefits?
en.displayIntent: Check domain-oriented module organization and maintainability reasoning.
guidance: Define a feature module by business capability.
Use a concrete module such as UsersModule.
Explain cohesion, ownership, and explicit exports.
commonMistakes: Splitting only by technical layer.
Exporting everything from each feature.
Treating modules as folders only.

vi.title: Feature Module
vi.displayQuestion: Feature Module trong NestJS là gì và lợi ích của nó là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra tư duy tổ chức module theo domain/feature và maintainability.
guidance: Định nghĩa feature module theo business capability.
Dùng ví dụ cụ thể như UsersModule.
Giải thích cohesion, ownership và explicit exports.
commonMistakes: Chỉ split theo technical layer.
Export mọi thứ từ mỗi feature.
Xem module chỉ là folder.

ja.title: Feature Module
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS の Feature Module とは何で、その利点は何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: domain-oriented module organization と maintainability の理解を確認する。
guidance: business capability に基づいて feature module を定義する。
UsersModule のような具体例を使う。
cohesion、ownership、explicit exports を説明する。
commonMistakes: technical layer だけで分割する。
各 feature からすべてを export する。
module を folder としてだけ扱う。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Feature modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for feature module structure, AppModule imports, and module organization.

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands exports in @Module() as the public API of a module for dependency injection across module boundaries.
primaryQuestion: What is the purpose of exports in @Module()?
expectedSignals: Defines exports as the set of providers or modules made available to modules that import this module.
Explains that providers are encapsulated by default and must be exported to be injected elsewhere.
Clarifies that exporting a provider does not automatically register it in unrelated modules; consumers still import the exporting module.
Mentions re-exporting an imported module when building a CoreModule or SharedModule style aggregator.
Frames exports as the module's public API and recommends keeping it minimal.
redFlags: Thinks exports are for JavaScript/TypeScript module syntax only.
Says controllers need to be exported for their routes to work.
Exports every provider from every module by default.
Cannot explain why an imported module's internal provider may still be unavailable.

scoringHints.description: Explains module encapsulation, provider public API, consumer imports, re-exporting, and minimal exports.
Understands exports for sharing providers but misses re-export or minimal API judgment.
Knows exports are for sharing but cannot explain the importing module relationship.
Confuses Nest module exports with TypeScript exports or route mounting.

followUps.question: If PaymentsModule imports OrdersModule, can PaymentsService inject OrdersRepository if OrdersModule does not export it?
What would you export from UsersModule: UsersService, UsersController, DTOs, or repositories? Why?
What is the downside of exporting every provider from a feature module?

en.title: Purpose of module exports
en.displayQuestion: What is the purpose of exports in @Module()?
en.displayIntent: Check whether exports are understood as a module's public injectable API.
guidance: Explain provider encapsulation by default.
Describe exports plus imports as the sharing mechanism.
Mention minimal public API and re-exporting when useful.
commonMistakes: Confusing Nest exports with TypeScript exports.
Exporting controllers for route mounting.
Exporting everything by default.

vi.title: Mục đích của exports trong Module
vi.displayQuestion: Mục đích của exports trong @Module() là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu exports là public injectable API của module hay không.
guidance: Giải thích provider mặc định bị encapsulate trong module.
Mô tả exports cộng với imports là cơ chế share provider.
Nhắc tới public API tối thiểu và re-export khi phù hợp.
commonMistakes: Nhầm Nest exports với TypeScript exports.
Export controller để mount route.
Export mọi thứ mặc định.

ja.title: module exports の目的
ja.displayQuestion: @Module() の exports は何のためにありますか。
ja.displayIntent: exports を module の public injectable API として理解しているか確認する。
guidance: provider はデフォルトで module 内に encapsulate されると説明する。
exports と imports が provider 共有の仕組みだと説明する。
最小限の public API と必要な re-export に触れる。
commonMistakes: Nest exports と TypeScript exports を混同する。
route mount のために controller を export する。
デフォルトですべてを export する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Module exports
sourceReferences.note: Used for module exports, shared modules, and re-exporting.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands dynamic module configuration patterns, especially synchronous forRoot() versus DI-powered asynchronous forRootAsync().
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between forRoot() and forRootAsync()?
expectedSignals: Explains that forRoot() typically configures a dynamic module synchronously with directly supplied options.
Explains that forRootAsync() builds configuration asynchronously or through Nest dependency injection.
Mentions useFactory, useClass, or useExisting patterns for async configuration.
Gives examples such as loading database options from ConfigService, secrets manager, or environment-dependent providers.
Recognizes that forRoot is usually application-wide configuration, while forFeature often registers feature-scoped pieces that rely on root configuration.
redFlags: Says forRootAsync is only for returning a Promise and misses dependency injection.
Uses forRoot in feature modules repeatedly for singleton infrastructure without considering duplicate configuration.
Hard-codes process.env in reusable modules instead of injecting configuration.
Cannot explain what a DynamicModule returns.

scoringHints.description: Explains dynamic modules, sync versus DI/async configuration, useFactory/useClass/useExisting, and when root configuration should be centralized.
Understands sync versus async options but misses provider-token or forFeature nuance.
Recognizes method names from libraries but cannot explain the underlying dynamic module pattern.
Cannot distinguish forRoot() from forRootAsync() in practical NestJS configuration.

followUps.question: How would you configure TypeOrmModule using ConfigService instead of a hard-coded object?
What can go wrong if multiple feature modules call forRoot() on the same infrastructure module?
followUps.purpose: Probe duplicate configuration and lifecycle awareness.

followUps.question: What does a dynamic module return, and where do its providers and exports come from?

en.title: forRoot versus forRootAsync
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between forRoot() and forRootAsync() in NestJS?
en.displayIntent: Check dynamic module configuration and DI-powered async options.
guidance: Explain synchronous options passed to forRoot().
Explain async or DI-based options passed to forRootAsync().
Mention useFactory, useClass, useExisting, and ConfigService examples.
commonMistakes: Thinking async only means Promise.
Calling forRoot repeatedly in feature modules without considering lifecycle.
Hard-coding environment access inside reusable modules.

vi.title: forRoot và forRootAsync
vi.displayQuestion: forRoot() và forRootAsync() trong NestJS khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về dynamic module configuration và async options dùng DI.
guidance: Giải thích forRoot() nhận options đồng bộ.
Giải thích forRootAsync() nhận options async hoặc thông qua DI.
Nhắc tới useFactory, useClass, useExisting và ví dụ ConfigService.
commonMistakes: Tưởng async chỉ có nghĩa là Promise.
Gọi forRoot lặp lại ở feature module mà không nghĩ tới lifecycle.
Hard-code env access trong reusable module.

ja.title: forRoot と forRootAsync
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS の forRoot() と forRootAsync() の違いは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: dynamic module configuration と DI を使った async options を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: forRoot() に同期的な options を渡すことを説明する。
forRootAsync() が async または DI-based options を使うことを説明する。
useFactory、useClass、useExisting、ConfigService の例に触れる。
commonMistakes: async は Promise だけの意味だと思う。
lifecycle を考えず feature module で forRoot を繰り返す。
reusable module の中で env access を hard-code する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Dynamic modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for forRoot, forRootAsync, registerAsync, ConfigurableModuleBuilder, and dynamic module conventions.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands that modules should not import themselves and can reason about circular module dependency problems instead of hiding them.
primaryQuestion: Can a Module import itself? What happens?
expectedSignals: States that a module should not import itself because it creates an invalid or circular module relationship.
Explains that Nest builds a module graph from imports and self-imports can lead to circular dependency or module resolution errors.
Mentions that the underlying design issue is usually confused ownership or misplaced shared dependency.
Suggests extracting shared providers into a separate module or correcting which module imports which dependency.
Can distinguish a self-import from valid re-exporting or from importing another module that exports needed providers.
redFlags: Says self-import is a normal way to access the module's own providers.
Uses forwardRef reflexively without understanding the dependency direction.
Confuses TypeScript circular imports with Nest module graph imports and cannot inspect either.
Cannot suggest a redesign when module imports become circular.

scoringHints.description: Rejects self-import, explains module graph/circular dependency risk, and proposes extracting or correcting boundaries.
Knows self-import is wrong but only partially explains why or how to fix the design.
Recognizes an error may happen but relies on forwardRef or trial-and-error fixes.
Cannot reason about module import cycles.

followUps.question: If OrdersModule imports PaymentsModule and PaymentsModule imports OrdersModule, what design questions would you ask first?
When is forwardRef acceptable, and why should it not be your first design choice?
What shared provider could be moved into a separate module to break a cycle?

en.title: Self-importing modules
en.displayQuestion: Can a NestJS Module import itself? What happens?
en.displayIntent: Check module graph and circular dependency reasoning.
guidance: State that self-import is not a valid design pattern.
Explain the module graph or circular dependency problem.
Suggest extracting shared providers or correcting ownership.
commonMistakes: Using self-import to access local providers.
Using forwardRef without redesigning boundaries.
Confusing TypeScript imports with Nest module imports.

vi.title: Module tự import chính nó
vi.displayQuestion: Một Module trong NestJS có thể import chính nó không? Chuyện gì sẽ xảy ra?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng hiểu module graph và circular dependency.
guidance: Nói rõ self-import không phải pattern hợp lệ.
Giải thích vấn đề module graph hoặc circular dependency.
Đề xuất tách shared provider hoặc sửa lại ownership.
commonMistakes: Dùng self-import để truy cập provider nội bộ.
Dùng forwardRef mà không xem lại boundary.
Nhầm TypeScript import với Nest module import.

ja.title: module の self-import
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS の Module は自分自身を import できますか。何が起きますか。
ja.displayIntent: module graph と circular dependency の理解を確認する。
guidance: self-import は有効な設計 pattern ではないと述べる。
module graph または circular dependency の問題を説明する。
shared provider の抽出や ownership の修正を提案する。
commonMistakes: local provider にアクセスするために self-import を使う。
boundary を見直さず forwardRef を使う。
TypeScript import と Nest module import を混同する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for module imports, module graph, and module encapsulation concepts.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Circular dependency
sourceReferences.note: Used for circular dependency concepts and forwardRef caution.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands how Nest uses controller and route decorator metadata, plus module registration, to build the routing map.
primaryQuestion: How does NestJS know which Controller handles which route?
expectedSignals: Explains that decorators such as @Controller(), @Get(), @Post(), @Put(), and @Delete() attach route metadata to classes and methods.
Mentions that controllers must be listed in a module's controllers array for Nest to discover and instantiate them.
Explains that Nest scans module metadata during bootstrap and builds a routing map from controller prefix plus method-level route metadata.
Connects HTTP method and path matching to the selected handler method.
Notes that route order and parameterized routes can matter when routes overlap.
redFlags: Thinks Nest discovers every class ending in Controller automatically from the filesystem.
Ignores module registration in the controllers array.
Cannot explain how @Controller('users') and @Get(':id') combine.
Confuses routing with dependency injection provider resolution.

scoringHints.description: Explains decorator metadata, module controller registration, bootstrap scanning, route map construction, and handler selection.
Understands decorators and module registration but misses bootstrap scanning or overlapping route nuance.
Knows route decorators by name but not how Nest discovers controllers.
Cannot explain NestJS route-to-controller resolution.

followUps.question: What happens if UsersController has decorators but is not added to any module's controllers array?
How does Nest combine @Controller('users') and @Get(':id') during route mapping?
Why should static routes usually be declared before parameterized routes when paths overlap?

en.title: Route-to-controller resolution
en.displayQuestion: How does NestJS know which Controller handles which route?
en.displayIntent: Check decorator metadata, module discovery, and routing map understanding.
guidance: Explain controller and method decorators as metadata.
Mention controllers array registration in a module.
Describe bootstrap scanning and route map creation.
commonMistakes: Assuming filesystem auto-discovery.
Forgetting controller module registration.
Confusing routing with provider injection.

vi.title: Nest map route tới Controller
vi.displayQuestion: NestJS biết Controller nào xử lý route nào bằng cách nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về decorator metadata, module discovery và routing map.
guidance: Giải thích controller và method decorator là metadata.
Nhắc tới việc đăng ký controller trong controllers array của module.
Mô tả bootstrap scanning và quá trình tạo route map.
commonMistakes: Tưởng Nest tự scan filesystem.
Quên đăng ký controller trong module.
Nhầm routing với provider injection.

ja.title: route と controller の解決
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS はどの Controller がどの route を処理するかをどう判断しますか。
ja.displayIntent: decorator metadata、module discovery、routing map を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: controller と method decorator が metadata であることを説明する。
module の controllers array への登録に触れる。
bootstrap scanning と route map 作成を説明する。
commonMistakes: filesystem から自動 discovery されると思う。
controller の module registration を忘れる。
routing と provider injection を混同する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Controllers
sourceReferences.note: Used for controller routing metadata, module registration, and route mapping.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for controllers array and module metadata.
